title: [VPlan] Factor collectGroupedReplicateMemOps
author: artagnon
description: Factor collectGroupedReplicateMemOps, enabling the reuse of canHoistOrSinkWithNoAliasCheck in hoistInvariantLoads.

tests.test_body:
; Verify that `-vplan-print-after-all` option works.


; Also verify that VPlans are actually printed (we aren't interested in the
; exact dump content, just that it's performed):

;
;

define void @foo(ptr %ptr, i64 %n) {
entry:
  br label %header

header:
  %iv = phi i64 [ 0, %entry ], [ %iv.next, %header ]
  %gep = getelementptr i64, ptr %ptr, i64 %iv
  store i64 %iv, ptr %gep
  %iv.next = add nsw i64 %iv, 1
  %exitcond = icmp slt i64 %iv.next, %n
  br i1 %exitcond, label %header, label %exit

exit:
  ret void
}

comments.author: artagnon
comments.body:
> I don't know how ScopedAA works. Would the following be ok?
> 
> ```llvm
>   %store i16 42, %p + 2
>   %ld i16, %p       ; No alias with store, earliest load
>   %ld i32, %p       ; Another load in the group, this one is partial alias
> ```

Good question! We really should be using the full AliasAnalysis in VPlan -- ScopedNoAliasAA is just one small component of AA that we're currently using for simplicity, which relies on the noalias-metadata; this is very limited indeed. See https://github.com/artagnon/llvm-project/blob/main/llvm/test/Transforms/LoopVectorize/hoist-predicated-loads-with-predicated-stores.ll -- there are plenty of missed optimizations, fixing them requires us to use the full AA; I plan to investigate this problem later. At the moment, I just want to us to use the current ScopedNoAliasAA code to the maximum extent possible -- I've hence factored out collectGroupedReplicateMemOps, so we can reuse it. The current logic surrounding the final alias call has a lot of firepower, and is unfortunately being limited by the final alias call.

For your example, it's impossible to tell if it will be analyzed by ScopedNoAliasAA without creating a full example, and running it through the AA metadata-annotater.
